extra.reasoning:
The property requires globally(opted_out implies not sent). Transition t5 violates this: on send_attempt, if (opted_out and ticks >= 12), it sets sent := true while opted_out is still true. This produces a reachable state with opted_out=true AND sent=true.

Counterexample (14 events, within the 16-event bound):
1. optout (t1): opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2-13. Twelve tick events (t4): each increments ticks while ticks<12; starting from 0, after 12 ticks, ticks=12. sent stays false.
14. send_attempt: guard of t5 (opted_out and ticks>=12) holds, so t5 fires and sets sent := true. State is now opted_out=true, sent=true.

At step 14 the state has opted_out=true and sent=true, so opted_out implies not sent is false — the global property is violated. (Even though t3's guard 'opted_out' also holds on this event, t5 is enabled and produces the violating behavior; the system is unsafe under any rule-ordering that allows t5 to fire.)
